data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T224511-8bd80742/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T224511-8bd80742/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T224511-8bd80742/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T224511-8bd80742/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T224511-8bd80742/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-6ecd59aa">
    <title>work: preflight project-local skills before long-running entrypoints</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Long-running DDx entrypoints can start work even when the project-local skill install is broken. In /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, post-attempt triage logged `triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle (continuing)` only after an execution failed. The operator had to infer that the project still had a pre-migration skill layout and needed `ddx update --force` / `ddx doctor`.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/cmd/work.go:106` delegates directly to `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl` without a lightweight runtime preflight. `cli/cmd/try.go:102-120` resolves execution flags and proceeds toward store/runtime setup without checking project-local skill prerequisites. `cli/cmd/server.go:98-146` starts the server without surfacing degraded project-local skill layout. `cli/cmd/doctor.go:281-287` already detects legacy skill symlinks and prints `run: ddx update --force`, but that diagnostic is only available when the operator explicitly runs `ddx doctor`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a shared lightweight project-runtime preflight helper for long-running entrypoints. It should reuse existing doctor/install helper logic where practical, but must not run the full `ddx doctor` checks.
- Wire the helper into `ddx work`, `ddx try`, compatibility `ddx agent execute-loop`, and `ddx server` before execution begins.
- For work/try/execute-loop: fail on missing required project/tracker state, warn once per process for optional lifecycle-hook degradation in WARN-ONLY mode, and include remediation.
- For server: fail on required server project state, but log degraded startup diagnostics for missing lifecycle skills or legacy skill symlink layout.
- Checked paths for `bead-lifecycle` must be `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` under the resolved project root.
- Remediation text must include `ddx update --force` first, then `ddx doctor`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add network, package-manager, route/provider, stale-doc, or Fizeau health probes to this preflight.
- Do not change bead-lifecycle hook classification semantics.
- Do not make `ddx server` refuse to start solely because `bead-lifecycle` is missing.
- Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually; tracker mutation is handled by this bead record only.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a9d130d0. No deps.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestProjectRuntimePreflight_MissingBeadLifecycleReportsCheckedPathsAndRemediation` verifies a project with `.agents/skills/ddx/SKILL.md` but no nested `bead-lifecycle` reports the resolved project root, both checked nested skill paths, `ddx update --force`, and `ddx doctor`.
2. `TestWorkPreflight_WarnsOnceForMissingLifecycleSkill` verifies `ddx work --once` emits one preflight warning before dispatch and does not repeat the warning across loop iterations in the same process.
3. `TestTryPreflight_WarnsBeforeClaimForMissingLifecycleSkill` verifies `ddx try <id>` runs the preflight before claim/worktree setup and includes the remediation text.
4. `TestServerPreflight_MissingLifecycleSkillStartsDegraded` verifies `ddx server` surfaces degraded startup diagnostics without blocking server construction/startup solely because `bead-lifecycle` is missing.
5. `TestProjectRuntimePreflight_LegacySkillSymlinkSuggestsUpdateForce` verifies legacy project-local skill symlinks reuse the FEAT-015 remediation path: `ddx update --force`, then `ddx doctor`.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run 'Test(ProjectRuntimePreflight|WorkPreflight|TryPreflight|ServerPreflight)'` green.
7. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:cli, area:agent, area:server, area:skills, kind:feature, reliability, bead-quality, spec:FEAT-010, spec:FEAT-015</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-a9d130d0" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="f54eda56b6ac709e39c18eddaaf2d16178a50818" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T224511-8bd80742"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented the shared project-runtime preflight and wired it into the long-running entrypoints, but I did not commit because the required broad package gate is still red on pre-existing `execute-bead` tests.

What changed:
- Added a shared lightweight preflight helper in [cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6ecd59aa-20260506T224511-8bd80742/cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go:31).
- Wired it into `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-loop` in [cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6ecd59aa-20260506T224511-8bd80742/cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1597).
- Wired it into `ddx try` in [cli/cmd/try.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6ecd59aa-20260506T224511-8bd80742/cli/cmd/try.go:102).
- Wired it into `ddx server` in [cli/cmd/server.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6ecd59aa-20260506T224511-8bd80742/cli/cmd/server.go:98).
- Added a test seam for server startup in [cli/cmd/command_factory.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6ecd59aa-20260506T224511-8bd80742/cli/cmd/command_factory.go:81).
- Added focused tests in [cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6ecd59aa-20260506T224511-8bd80742/cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight_test.go:26).

Verified green:
- `go test ./cmd -run 'Test(ProjectRuntimePreflight|WorkPreflight|TryPreflight|ServerPreflight)'`
- `go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server -run 'Test(ProjectRuntimePreflight|WorkPreflight|TryPreflight|ServerPreflight)'`

Blocked:
- `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` still fails on existing `execute-bead` coverage, including `TestExecuteBeadContextBudgetFromConfig`, `TestInvokeExecuteBeadFromLoopParsesJSONAmidWarnings`, and `TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidence*`.

I recorded the blocker in [.ddx/executions/20260506T224511-8bd80742/no_changes_rationale.txt](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6ecd59aa-20260506T224511-8bd80742/.ddx/executions/20260506T224511-8bd80742/no_changes_rationale.txt:1).
